claim.observation: fls -r -l of inode 69647 (C:\temp) on the DC NTFS volume lists two subdirectories created 2018-09-05: 'Active Directory' containing 'ntds.dit' (67,108,864 bytes) and 'ntds.jfm' (16,384 bytes), and 'registry' containing 'SYSTEM' (16,252,928 bytes) and 'SECURITY' (65,536 bytes).
claim.interpretation: The co-located set ntds.dit + ntds.jfm (the Active Directory database and its ESE checkpoint) together with the SYSTEM and SECURITY registry hives, staged under C:\temp\Active Directory and C:\temp\registry, is the canonical on-disk output of an 'ntdsutil ... ifm create full c:\temp' (Install-From-Media) operation. This is a domain-controller credential-theft artifact: ntds.dit holds every domain account's password hash and the SYSTEM hive supplies the boot key needed to decrypt them. The folders are dated 2018-09-05, well after DC provisioning.

evidence_pointer.artifact: C:\temp (NTFS root) — Active Directory\ntds.dit, registry\SYSTEM, registry\SECURITY
evidence_pointer.locator: inode 69647 (C:\temp); ntds.dit inode 132832; SYSTEM inode 132833; SECURITY inode 132834; created 2018-09-05 12:16-12:17 UTC
